The min-cut problem and min s-t cut are related concepts, but the techniques for solving min-cut do not extend to min s-t cut due to the exponential number of min s-t cuts for fixed terminals.

While there exist polynomially many min-cuts in any graph, there can be exponentially many min s-t cuts for fixed terminals, highlighting the difference in the number of possible solutions for these two concepts.
